name: Easy Cherry Pudding
recipeIngredient: Canned pitted cherries with syrup (1 can, 1 pound)
Additional granulated sugar for topping (1/2 cup)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Melt the margarine and pour it into a baking pan.
3. In a separate bowl, mix together the flour, sugar (only 1 cup), baking powder, and milk or water until combined.
4. Pour the flour mixture onto the melted margarine in the baking pan.
5. Carefully pour the canned cherries with syrup over the flour mixture.
6. Sprinkle the additional granulated sugar evenly over the top of the pudding.
7. Bake for 1 hour in the preheated oven.
8. Serve hot or cold. If served hot, ice cream can be added as desired.
Variations:
- Any canned fruit can be used, such as plums (for plum pudding), pineapple, etc. Ensure any pitted fruits have their pits removed before use.
- Fresh fruit should not be used for this recipe, as it is intended for canned fruit only.
